[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, after a maker signs a Merkle root containing a buy offer at tick T, delay execution until block.timestamp crosses a settlement-fee breakpoint boundary (e.g., TTM drops from just above 1 day to just below 1 day), causing the settlement fee to jump from settlementFeeCbp1 to settlementFeeCbp0 interpolation, so that claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] increases by a different amount than the maker expected when signing, under PRECONDITIONS: market with non-zero settlementFeeCbp0 != settlementFeeCbp1, offer signed with EcrecoverRatifier, taker holds the valid Merkle proof and waits for TTM boundary crossing, CALL_SEQUENCE: [sign Merkle root at TTM > 1 day, warp to TTM < 1 day, call take() with valid ratifierData], violating INVARIANT: claimableSettlementFee increase equals maker's expected fee at signing time, causing scoped impact: settlement fee overclaimed or underclaimed relative to maker's signed intent? Proof idea: fuzz (settlementFeeCbp0, settlementFeeCbp1, units, tick) across the 1-day TTM boundary and assert delta claimableSettlementFee matches fee at signing TTM.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, given a Merkle tree containing a sell offer (offer.buy == false) at tick T signed by the maker, execute the offer at TTM = 0 (post-maturity) where settlementFee = settlementFeeCbp0 * CBP, causing sellerAssets = units.mulDivUp(offerPrice, WAD) and buyerAssets = units.mulDivUp(offerPrice + settlementFeeCbp0*CBP, WAD), so that the seller-maker receives less than offerPrice per unit while the fee is charged to the buyer-taker, under PRECONDITIONS: market past maturity, settlementFeeCbp0 > 0, sell offer signed with EcrecoverRatifier, CALL_SEQUENCE: [warp past maturity, call take() with valid ratifierData], violating INVARIANT: seller-maker receives exactly offerPrice per unit as encoded in the signed offer, causing scoped impact: settlement fee charged to wrong side (seller-maker bears implicit cost via reduced net proceeds)? Proof idea: unit test with settlementFeeCbp0 = maxSettlementFee(0), sell offer at tick 0, assert sellerAssets < units.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the fact that EcrecoverRatifier validates offer.tick but not the resulting settlement fee, by taking a buy offer (offer.buy == true) where offerPrice - settlementFee(id, TTM) rounds to zero (sellerPrice = 0), causing sellerAssets = 0 and claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] += buyerAssets - 0 = buyerAssets, effectively routing the entire buyer payment as settlement fee with zero assets transferred to the seller, under PRECONDITIONS: buy offer with tick such that tickToPrice(tick) == settlementFee(id, TTM), valid Merkle proof, CALL_SEQUENCE: [set settlement fee to max, sign buy offer at low tick, take()], violating INVARIANT: seller receives non-zero assets for non-zero units taken, causing scoped impact: settlement fee overclaimed (entire payment captured as fee, seller receives nothing)? Proof idea: fuzz (tick, settlementFeeCbp, TTM) to find tick where offerPrice == settlementFee and assert sellerAssets == 0.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, given a Merkle tree with a buy offer using maxAssets (offer.maxAssets > 0), cause the offer to be consumed faster than the maker intended by executing at a higher settlement fee (after fee setter increases it), because consumed[maker][group] tracks buyerAssets = units.mulDivDown(offerPrice, WAD) for buy offers with maxAssets, and higher settlement fee means higher buyerPrice means higher buyerAssets per unit, exhausting maxAssets sooner, under PRECONDITIONS: buy offer with maxAssets set, fee setter increases settlementFeeCbp between signing and execution, valid Merkle proof, CALL_SEQUENCE: [sign offer with maxAssets, feeSetter increases settlement fee, taker calls take()], violating INVARIANT: offer capacity in units is not reduced by settlement fee changes after signing, causing scoped impact: settlement fee change causes underclaim of units (maker's offer exhausted before intended unit capacity)? Proof idea: fuzz (maxAssets, units, settlementFee before/after) and assert consumed units differ.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, given a Merkle tree with a sell offer using maxAssets (offer.maxAssets > 0), cause the offer to be consumed slower than the maker intended when settlement fee decreases, because consumed[maker][group] tracks sellerAssets = units.mulDivUp(offerPrice, WAD) for sell offers with maxAssets, and lower settlement fee means lower buyerPrice but sellerAssets is unchanged (sellerPrice = offerPrice for sell offers), so the consumption tracking is unaffected by settlement fee for sell offers with maxAssets, under PRECONDITIONS: sell offer with maxAssets, fee setter decreases settlement fee, CALL_SEQUENCE: [sign sell offer with maxAssets, feeSetter decreases fee, taker calls take()], violating INVARIANT: settlement fee changes do not affect offer consumption accounting for sell offers with maxAssets, causing scoped impact: settlement fee underclaimed (fee decreases but offer consumption unchanged, protocol collects less fee per unit)? Proof idea: differential test comparing claimableSettlementFee with high vs low settlement fee for sell offer with maxAssets.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, given a Merkle tree containing multiple offers at different ticks (e.g., offer0 at tick=0, offer1 at tick=MAX_TICK), selectively execute the offer with the lowest tick (lowest price) at a time when the settlement fee is highest, causing the buyer-taker to pay the most in settlement fees relative to the seller-maker's proceeds, under PRECONDITIONS: Merkle tree with offers at varying ticks, settlement fee at maximum for current TTM, CALL_SEQUENCE: [sign Merkle root with multiple offers, warp to TTM where fee is maximized, take lowest-tick offer], violating INVARIANT: settlement fee is proportional to offerPrice and does not disproportionately burden one side, causing scoped impact: settlement fee overclaimed relative to the value of the trade (fee exceeds intended proportion of trade value)? Proof idea: fuzz (tick, TTM, settlementFeeCbp) and assert (buyerAssets - sellerAssets) / buyerAssets <= maxSettlementFee(index) / WAD.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: cancelRoot / isRatified] Can an unprivileged taker front-run a maker's cancelRoot() transaction to execute a take() with a valid Merkle proof before the root is marked as isRootCanceled[maker][root] = true, thereby collecting settlement fees at a fee rate the maker was trying to avoid (e.g., fee just increased by feeSetter), under PRECONDITIONS: maker signed root at low fee, feeSetter increased fee, maker submits cancelRoot, taker front-runs with take(), CALL_SEQUENCE: [feeSetter increases fee, maker submits cancelRoot, taker front-runs with take() using valid proof], violating INVARIANT: maker can prevent execution at unfavorable fee rates by canceling the root, causing scoped impact: settlement fee overclaimed from maker's perspective (executed at higher fee than maker intended)? Proof idea: state-transition test with mempool ordering, assert take() succeeds before cancelRoot() in same block.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, given a Merkle tree signed by an authorized delegate (not the maker), execute the offer after the maker revokes the delegate's authorization via setIsAuthorized(delegate, false), but before the revocation is processed, causing the take to succeed with a settlement fee that the maker did not intend to authorize, under PRECONDITIONS: maker authorizes delegate, delegate signs Merkle root, maker revokes delegate, taker front-runs revocation with take(), CALL_SEQUENCE: [maker authorizes delegate, delegate signs root, maker submits revocation, taker front-runs with take()], violating INVARIANT: revoked authorization prevents execution of signed offers, causing scoped impact: settlement fee charged at unintended rate (maker's revocation bypassed)? Proof idea: unit test with authorization revocation race, assert take() reverts after revocation.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, given a Merkle tree with a buy offer where the maker is also the buyer (offer.buy == true, offer.maker == buyer), trigger continuous fee accrual for the buyer via _updatePosition() called inside take() (line 379: if hasCredit(id, buyer) || units > buyerPos.debt), causing continuousFeeCredit to increase by the accrued fee, which is then claimable by feeClaimer, effectively charging the buyer-maker a continuous fee that was not reflected in the signed offer's tick/price, under PRECONDITIONS: buyer-maker has existing credit with non-zero pendingFee, continuousFee > 0, valid Merkle proof, CALL_SEQUENCE: [buyer-maker accumulates credit and pendingFee, warp time, taker calls take() with buy offer], violating INVARIANT: take() does not charge continuous fees beyond what is crystallized in buyerPendingFeeIncrease for the new credit, causing scoped impact: continuous fee overclaimed (accrued fee from existing credit is crystallized into continuousFeeCredit during take, reducing buyer-maker's net credit)? Proof idea: fuzz (credit, pendingFee, elapsed, units) and assert continuousFeeCredit increase equals expected accrued fee.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, given a sell offer (offer.buy == false) where the seller-maker has existing credit, cause _updatePosition() to NOT be called for the seller when sellerPos.credit == 0 (line 380: if hasCredit(id, seller)), meaning the seller's accrued continuous fee is not crystallized into continuousFeeCredit, leaving the fee unclaimable until the seller's next interaction, under PRECONDITIONS: sell offer, seller has no credit (only debt), valid Merkle proof, CALL_SEQUENCE: [seller accumulates debt only, taker calls take() with sell offer], violating INVARIANT: continuous fee accrual is consistent regardless of whether _updatePosition is called, causing scoped impact: continuous fee underclaimed (accrued fee not crystallized into continuousFeeCredit when seller has no credit)? Proof idea: unit test with seller having only debt, assert continuousFeeCredit unchanged after take().

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, given a buy offer (offer.buy == true) where the buyer-maker has existing debt (buyerPos.debt >= units), cause _updatePosition() to NOT be called for the buyer (line 379: condition is false when !hasCredit(id, buyer) && units <= buyerPos.debt), so that the buyer's accrued continuous fee from any prior credit is not crystallized, and buyerPendingFeeIncrease is computed on buyerCreditIncrease = 0 (since units <= debt), resulting in zero new continuous fee being charged to the buyer for this take, under PRECONDITIONS: buy offer, buyer-maker has debt >= units and no credit, valid Merkle proof, CALL_SEQUENCE: [buyer-maker accumulates debt, taker calls take() with buy offer where units <= debt], violating INVARIANT: continuous fee accrual is triggered for all parties with outstanding credit obligations, causing scoped impact: continuous fee underclaimed (buyer's credit-reducing take skips _updatePosition, no fee crystallized)? Proof idea: unit test with buyer having debt >= units and no credit, assert continuousFeeCredit unchanged.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, given a Merkle tree with a buy offer at TTM = 0 (post-maturity), cause buyerPendingFeeIncrease = buyerCreditIncrease.mulDivDown(continuousFee * 0, WAD) = 0, meaning no continuous fee is crystallized for the buyer's new credit, even though the buyer now holds credit that will accrue fees until maturity (which has already passed, so accrualEnd = maturity = block.timestamp - delta), under PRECONDITIONS: market past maturity, buy offer with valid Merkle proof, buyer takes credit post-maturity, CALL_SEQUENCE: [warp past maturity, taker calls take() with buy offer], violating INVARIANT: continuous fee is correctly crystallized for all new credit regardless of TTM, causing scoped impact: continuous fee underclaimed (post-maturity credit creation skips continuous fee crystallization entirely)? Proof idea: unit test with post-maturity take, assert buyerPendingFeeIncrease == 0 and continuousFeeCredit unchanged for buyer's new credit.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, given a Merkle tree with a sell offer where the seller-maker has credit and pendingFee, cause sellerPendingFeeDecrease to be computed as mulDivUp(sellerPos.pendingFee, sellerCreditDecrease, sellerPos.credit) BEFORE _updatePosition is called (line 380 calls _updatePosition only if hasCredit, but sellerPos.credit and sellerPos.pendingFee are read from storage before the update), meaning the sellerPendingFeeDecrease uses stale (pre-accrual) pendingFee values, under PRECONDITIONS: sell offer, seller has credit and pendingFee, time has elapsed since last accrual, valid Merkle proof, CALL_SEQUENCE: [seller accumulates credit and pendingFee, warp time, taker calls take() with sell offer], violating INVARIANT: sellerPendingFeeDecrease is computed on post-update pendingFee values, causing scoped impact: continuous fee mis-accounting (sellerPendingFeeDecrease uses stale pendingFee, causing over- or under-decrease of seller's pending fee)? Proof idea: fuzz (credit, pendingFee, elapsed, units) and compare sellerPendingFeeDecrease with and without prior updatePosition call.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, given a Merkle tree with a sell offer where sellerPos.credit == sellerCreditDecrease (full credit consumed), cause sellerPendingFeeDecrease = mulDivUp(pendingFee, credit, credit) = pendingFee, zeroing out the seller's pendingFee, but the seller's credit is also zeroed, so the invariant pendingFee <= credit is maintained at 0 <= 0, yet the entire pendingFee is transferred to continuousFeeCredit via _updatePosition's accruedFee, potentially double-counting the fee (once via sellerPendingFeeDecrease and once via _updatePosition's accrual), under PRECONDITIONS: sell offer consuming all seller credit, seller has non-zero pendingFee, valid Merkle proof, CALL_SEQUENCE: [seller accumulates credit and pendingFee, taker calls take() consuming all seller credit], violating INVARIANT: continuous fee is not double-counted between sellerPendingFeeDecrease and _updatePosition accrual, causing scoped impact: continuous fee overclaimed (double-counting of seller's pending fee)? [File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, given a Merkle tree with a buy offer where the buyer-maker has existing credit (hasCredit returns true), trigger _updatePosition for the buyer which accrues the buyer's existing pendingFee into continuousFeeCredit, and then also add buyerPendingFeeIncrease for the new credit, causing continuousFeeCredit to increase by both the accrued existing fee AND the new crystallized fee in a single take(), under PRECONDITIONS: buy offer, buyer-maker has existing credit with pendingFee, continuousFee > 0, valid Merkle proof, CALL_SEQUENCE: [buyer-maker accumulates credit and pendingFee, warp time, taker calls take() with buy offer], violating INVARIANT: continuousFeeCredit increase per take equals only the accrued fee from existing positions plus new crystallization, causing scoped impact: continuous fee overclaimed (both accrual and crystallization happen in same take, feeClaimer can claim more than lenders owe)? [File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, given a Merkle tree with a buy offer, execute the offer via multicall() alongside a setMarketSettlementFee() call (if feeSetter is a contract that can be triggered), causing the settlement fee to change between the ratifier validation and the fee computation in take(), under PRECONDITIONS: feeSetter is a contract callable within multicall, buy offer with valid Merkle proof, CALL_SEQUENCE: [multicall([setMarketSettlementFee(id, index, newFee), take(offer, ratifierData, ...)])], violating INVARIANT: settlement fee used in take() matches the fee at the time of ratifier validation, causing scoped impact: settlement fee overclaimed or underclaimed (fee changed atomically with take execution)? [File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, given a Merkle tree with a buy offer where offer.callback is set to a contract that calls claimContinuousFee() during the onBuy callback, cause the continuousFeeCredit to be claimed before the take's state updates are finalized, potentially allowing the feeClaimer to claim fees that were just crystallized in the same take(), under PRECONDITIONS: buy offer with callback set to a contract that calls claimContinuousFee, feeClaimer == callback contract, valid Merkle proof, CALL_SEQUENCE: [take() -> onBuy callback -> claimContinuousFee()], violating INVARIANT: continuousFeeCredit claimed does not exceed fees actually accrued and crystallized, causing scoped impact: continuous fee overclaimed (callback claims fees crystallized in the same take before accounting is complete)? [File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, given a Merkle tree with a sell offer where offer.callback is set to a contract that calls claimSettlementFee() during the onSell callback, cause the claimableSettlementFee to be claimed before the transfer of buyerAssets - sellerAssets to the contract is complete (line 455: safeTransferFrom happens after the state update at line 418), under PRECONDITIONS: sell offer with callback calling claimSettlementFee, feeClaimer == callback contract, valid Merkle proof, CALL_SEQUENCE: [take() -> state update (line 418) -> onSell callback -> claimSettlementFee() -> safeTransferFrom], violating INVARIANT: claimableSettlementFee is backed by actual token balance in the contract, causing scoped impact: settlement fee unclaimable (feeClaimer claims fee before tokens arrive, subsequent claim fails due to insufficient balance)? [File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, given a Merkle tree with a buy offer using a fee-on-transfer loan token, cause the actual tokens received by the contract (buyerAssets - sellerAssets - transferFee) to be less than claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] += buyerAssets - sellerAssets, making the settlement fee unclaimable because the contract's token balance is insufficient, under PRECONDITIONS: loan token charges fee on transfer, buy offer with valid Merkle proof, CALL_SEQUENCE: [take() with fee-on-transfer token, claimSettlementFee()], violating INVARIANT: claimableSettlementFee[token] <= actual token balance attributable to settlement fees, causing scoped impact: settlement fee unclaimable (fee-on-transfer token causes claimableSettlementFee to exceed actual balance)? [File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, given a Merkle tree with a buy offer in a market where the same loan token is used across multiple markets, cause claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] to accumulate fees from market A while the actual token balance in the contract is shared with market B's withdrawable assets, making it possible for feeClaimer to claim settlement fees that are backed by lenders' withdrawable assets from market B rather than actual fee revenue, under PRECONDITIONS: two markets with same loan token, both have takes generating settlement fees, CALL_SEQUENCE: [take() in market A, take() in market B, claimSettlementFee(loanToken, amount, receiver)], violating INVARIANT: claimableSettlementFee[token] is backed by fee revenue not by lender withdrawable assets, causing scoped impact: settlement fee overclaimed (feeClaimer drains lender withdrawable assets via cross-market settlement fee claim)? Proof idea: invariant test asserting token balance >= withdrawable(marketA) + withdrawable(marketB) + claimableSettlementFee[token] + continuousFeeCredit(marketA) + continuousFeeCredit(marketB).

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, given a Merkle tree with a buy offer at TTM exactly at a breakpoint boundary (e.g., TTM == 1 days exactly), cause the settlement fee to be computed as settlementFeeCbp1 * CBP (the exact breakpoint value) rather than an interpolated value, and then execute the same offer one second later at TTM = 1 days - 1 second, causing the fee to be interpolated between cbp0 and cbp1, potentially resulting in a different fee than the maker expected, under PRECONDITIONS: buy offer signed at TTM == 1 days, taker delays by 1 second, valid Merkle proof, CALL_SEQUENCE: [sign at TTM == 1 days, warp 1 second, take()], violating INVARIANT: settlement fee at exact breakpoint equals the breakpoint value (no interpolation artifact), causing scoped impact: settlement fee mis-accounting at breakpoint boundary (1-second delay changes fee computation path)? Proof idea: unit test at TTM = 1 days vs TTM = 1 days - 1, assert fee continuity.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, given a Merkle tree with a buy offer in a market with TTM >= 360 days, cause the settlement fee to be fixed at settlementFeeCbp6 * CBP regardless of actual TTM, and then execute the offer at TTM = 361 days vs TTM = 1000 days, receiving the same settlement fee in both cases, potentially allowing the maker to sign offers far in advance knowing the fee is capped at the 360-day breakpoint, under PRECONDITIONS: market with maturity > 360 days from now, buy offer signed with EcrecoverRatifier, CALL_SEQUENCE: [sign offer at TTM > 360 days, take() at various TTM > 360 days], violating INVARIANT: settlement fee correctly reflects the 360-day cap for all TTM >= 360 days, causing scoped impact: settlement fee correctly capped (no mis-accounting, but verify the cap is applied consistently)? Proof idea: fuzz (TTM in [360 days, 100 years]) and assert settlementFee(id, TTM) == settlementFeeCbp6 * CBP.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, given a Merkle tree with a buy offer where the maker has authorized a delegate via setIsAuthorized, and the delegate signs a root containing offers with different ticks across multiple markets, cause the settlement fee to be computed differently per market (since each market has its own settlementFeeCbp values), allowing the taker to selectively execute the offer in the market with the highest settlement fee, maximizing claimableSettlementFee at the expense of the buyer-maker, under PRECONDITIONS: maker authorizes delegate, delegate signs root with offers in multiple markets, taker selects market with highest fee, CALL_SEQUENCE: [delegate signs multi-market root, taker calls take() in highest-fee market], violating INVARIANT: settlement fee is bounded by the market's configured caps and does not exceed maker's expected cost, causing scoped impact: settlement fee overclaimed (taker selects highest-fee market execution)? [File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, given a Merkle tree with a sell offer (offer.buy == false) where the seller-maker has credit, cause the sellerPendingFeeDecrease to be computed as mulDivUp(pendingFee, sellerCreditDecrease, sellerPos.credit) using the PRE-update credit value (before _updatePosition reduces credit via accrual), but _updatePosition is called first (line 380), so the credit used in the division is the POST-update credit, potentially causing a different sellerPendingFeeDecrease than if computed on pre-update values, under PRECONDITIONS: sell offer, seller has credit and pendingFee, time elapsed since last accrual, valid Merkle proof, CALL_SEQUENCE: [seller accumulates credit and pendingFee, warp time, taker calls take()], violating INVARIANT: sellerPendingFeeDecrease is proportional to the credit decrease relative to post-update credit, causing scoped impact: continuous fee mis-accounting (sellerPendingFeeDecrease computed on post-update credit, which is lower than pre-update, causing higher proportional decrease)? Proof idea: fuzz (credit, pendingFee, elapsed, units) and verify sellerPendingFeeDecrease uses post-update values.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, given a Merkle tree with a buy offer where the buyer-maker has both credit and debt (which should be impossible per the invariant 'a user never holds both credit and debt'), but if a rounding edge case allows credit > 0 and debt > 0 simultaneously, cause _updatePosition to be called (since hasCredit is true) and then buyerCreditIncrease = zeroFloorSub(units, buyerPos.debt) to be non-zero, leading to double-counting of continuous fee (accrual from existing credit + crystallization for new credit), under PRECONDITIONS: edge case where buyer has both credit and debt, buy offer with valid Merkle proof, CALL_SEQUENCE: [create edge case position, taker calls take()], violating INVARIANT: a user never holds both credit and debt simultaneously, causing scoped impact: continuous fee overclaimed (double fee accrual if invariant is violated)? Proof idea: invariant fuzz test asserting credit * debt == 0 for all users after any sequence of operations.
